greetingDefault: I know who you are. Strangers who ask too many questions are a category of person I watch closely. Speak.
greetingFriendly: You have been useful to the Church. That earns you my time. What do you need?
greetingHostile: You stand before an Inquisitor of the Universal Church. Choose your next words with great care.
farewellDefault: The Pancreator sees all things. Including this conversation.
unknownKeywordResponse: I have no interest in that topic. Ask me something relevant.

keywords.response: Harkan. Inquisitor, Second Rank, appointed by the See of Aragon. My authority on this world is second only to the Archbishop — who is conveniently off-world. So in practice, first.
I investigate. Heresy, tech violations, occult transgression. I determine what is clean and what is rot. Then I cut out the rot.

The Church names three great heresies: the worship of false gods, the unlicensed use of technology beyond your station, and the practice of unsanctioned occultism. I am concerned with all three on Aragon. Particularly the third.

Psi is tolerated — barely — when registered with the Church. Theurgy is holy, when practised by the ordained. Antinomy is never tolerated. There are signs of Antinomist activity in the wilderness. This concerns me greatly.

Antinomy is the occult of entropy — power drawn from the unravelling of order itself. It is not merely sinful. It is dangerous in a way most people cannot comprehend. Those who practise it do not age well.

The ruins beyond the city walls predate human settlement on Aragon. The Ur-Obun know them better than we do. Recently there have been lights at night — and two serfs have gone missing near the old stone circle. I need eyes I can trust out there.
If you are willing to investigate the stone circle and report what you find, I will ensure the Church opens certain doors for you. The Palace, specifically. The Baron listens to us when it suits him.

The Ur-Obun elder in the wilderness. I have spoken with her twice. She is... not an enemy. But she is not forthcoming. She knows what those ruins once were. I believe she is protecting that knowledge.

Technology beyond your feudal station is a violation of Church law. Energy weapons, advanced computing, unlicensed cybernetics. The League smuggles such things through this port constantly. Orlan and I understand each other — for now.
The Pancreator is not a question. The Pancreator is the answer. Questions are what I'm here for.
The Pancreator sees all things. Including this conversation.
